text.title: Shell Productivity Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Shell Productivity methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: install-cli-tools.sh MUST converge on re-run — every installed tool is detected first; missing tools are installed; existing tools are skipped.
rule.rationale: Shell config drifts; re-running the installer must not duplicate entries in ~/.bashrc.

rule.statement: Generated ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc carries a header comment with the ShellPlan version + last_reviewed date; the installer overwrites only when the header matches an earlier version.
rule.rationale: Header guards against clobbering hand-edits made outside the methodology.

rule.statement: ~/.bashrc / ~/.zshrc MUST NOT contain `export <SECRET>=...`. Secrets live in `~/.config/<svc>.env` sourced conditionally.
rule.rationale: Shell rc files leak via process listings + accidental shares.

rule.statement: Every ShellPlan has a named operator owner. 'team' / 'we' / '' are forbidden.
Shell history MUST be configured with `HISTSIZE=10000 HISTFILESIZE=20000`, `HISTCONTROL=ignoredups:erasedups`, and `shopt -s histappend` for bash (equivalent for zsh).
rule.rationale: Default history loses commands on parallel sessions; shared history is the productivity lever.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-shell-productivity.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
